title: Tables in Streams?
author: MatthewPinheiro
body:
Hello,

I didn't find record of this anywhere in the documentation or looking through some of the issues here, but apologies if someone has already asked about this.

Is it expected that Worksheets created from a Workbook Stream don't have an `.addTable` function? I'm using version 4.1.1. If this is expected, are there any plans to add that functionality? I'm trying to create a scalable process by utilizing streams for its memory advantages, and imo tables are a _huge_ part of Excel's usefulness for easy data manipulation, so I was surprised to see this isn't supported.

For reference, I'm initializing the worksheet I'd add the table to with the following code:
```
    var workbook = new Excel.stream.xlsx.WorkbookWriter();
    var worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet();

    worksheet.addTable // undefined
```

comments.author: pconnell99
comments.body: I second this.  There is no support for tables in the streaming system, but I need to be able to build tables in a streaming fashion?  I don't know enough about the format of the files to know if this is feasible.  Is there another way we can achieve this?  If addTable() didn't overwrite existing content but allowed it to be applied to a range, we could reload and apply the table over the top of the streamed rows.

comments.author: paeolo
comments.body: Any update on this? This feature is really valuable and it would great to have it in exceljs. At least the docs should mention that's it's not present in the streaming version of exceljs.

comments.author: YukiGibson
comments.body:
I wasted quite a lot of time trying to debug why this was failing for me. In TS, the signature of `Worksheet` was still showing the `addTable()` method. Obviously I got:
```
TypeError: worksheet.addTable is not a function
```
An unpleasant surprise to be honest when I searched any issue about it.

comments.author: alvaro-escalante
comments.body: I have the same problem, I am facing 502 from the server in production trying to write heavy excel files, and want to use `Excel.stream.xlsx`, but I am also using `worksheet.addTable()` and it fails.

comments.author: mrawdon
comments.body: @alvaro-escalante #1885 should address this issue if it gets merged in

comments.author: atignola
comments.body: Is there a scheduled release to include the fix from @mrawdon for this issue? The latest available version was released in August of last year.
